subtopics.content: Understand and use the structure of mathematical proof, proceeding from given assumptions through a series of logical steps to a conclusion
Use proof by deduction, e.g. using completion of the square, prove that n² − 6n + 10 is positive for all values of n; differentiation from first principles for small positive integer powers of x
Use proof by exhaustion; trying all the options for a finite set of cases
Use disproof by counter-example to show that a statement is untrue
Understand and use the laws of indices: a^m × a^n = a^(m+n), a^m ÷ a^n = a^(m−n), (a^m)^n = a^(mn); equivalence of a^(m/n) and ⁿ√(a^m)
Use and manipulate surds, including rationalising the denominator; simplify algebraic surds using (√x)² = x, √(xy) = √x√y and (√x + √y)(√x − √y) = x − y

Work with quadratic functions and their graphs; the discriminant b² − 4ac including conditions for real and repeated roots; completing the square; solution by factorisation, formula and completing the square
Solve simultaneous equations in two variables by elimination and substitution, including one linear and one quadratic equation

Solve linear and quadratic inequalities in a single variable, interpret graphically, including inequalities with brackets and fractions; express solutions using 'and', 'or', set notation; represent linear and quadratic inequalities graphically
Manipulate polynomials algebraically, including expanding brackets, collecting like terms, factorisation and simple algebraic division; use of the factor theorem; division by (ax+b) or (ax−b)

Understand and use graphs of functions; sketch curves defined by simple equations including polynomials, y=a/x, y=a/x² (including vertical and horizontal asymptotes); interpret algebraic solutions graphically; use intersection points to solve equations; understand proportional relationships
Understand the effect of simple transformations on y=f(x): y=af(x), y=f(x)+a, y=f(x+a), y=f(ax); sketch associated graphs
Understand and use the equation of a straight line: y−y₁=m(x−x₁), ax+by+c=0; gradient conditions for parallel and perpendicular lines; use straight line models in context

Coordinate geometry of the circle: (x−a)²+(y−b)²=r² and x²+y²+2fx+2gy+c=0; completing the square to find centre and radius; angle in semicircle, perpendicular from centre bisects chord, radius perpendicular to tangent; find equation of tangent
Understand and use the binomial expansion of (a+bx)ⁿ for positive integer n; notations n! and nCr; Pascal's triangle; relation between binomial coefficients; link to binomial probabilities
Understand and use definitions of sine, cosine and tangent for all arguments; sine and cosine rules; area of triangle in the form ½ab sin C; including ambiguous case of sine rule
Understand and use sine, cosine and tangent functions; their graphs, symmetries and periodicity
Understand and use tan θ = sin θ / cos θ and sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
Solve simple trigonometric equations in a given interval, including quadratic equations in sin, cos and tan and equations involving multiples of the unknown angle; answers in degrees
Know and use aˣ and its graph; know and use eˣ and its graph; gradient of e^(kx) equals ke^(kx); exponential model suitability
Know and use logₐx as inverse of aˣ; know and use lnx and its graph; lnx as inverse of eˣ; solution of equations e^(ax+b)=p and ln(ax+b)=q
Understand and use laws: logₐx + logₐy = logₐ(xy), logₐx − logₐy = logₐ(x/y), k·logₐx = logₐ(xᵏ)
Solve equations of the form aˣ = b; use change of base formula
Use logarithmic graphs to estimate parameters in relationships y=axⁿ and y=kbˣ
Understand and use exponential growth and decay in modelling; consideration of limitations and refinements
Understand derivative as gradient of tangent; dy/dx as rate of change; sketch gradient function; second derivatives; differentiation from first principles for small positive integer powers
Differentiate xⁿ for rational n, and related constant multiples, sums and differences
Apply differentiation to find gradients, tangents and normals, maxima and minima and stationary points; identify increasing/decreasing functions
Know and use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus; integration as reverse of differentiation; constant of integration
Integrate xⁿ (excluding n=−1) and related sums, differences and constant multiples; find equation of curve given f'(x) and a point
Evaluate definite integrals; use definite integral to find area under a curve and area bounded by a curve and given straight lines
Use vectors in two dimensions; column vectors and i, j unit vectors
Calculate magnitude and direction of a vector; convert between component form and magnitude/direction form; find unit vector
Add vectors diagrammatically and algebraically; multiplication by scalars; triangle and parallelogram laws; parallel vectors
Understand and use position vectors; calculate distance between two points represented by position vectors
Use vectors to solve problems in pure mathematics and in context, including forces
Understand and use terms 'population' and 'sample'; use samples to make informal inferences about population; advantages and disadvantages of census vs sample
Understand and use sampling techniques: simple random, stratified, systematic, quota, opportunity (convenience) sampling; select or critique sampling techniques
Interpret diagrams for single-variable data: histograms (area represents frequency), frequency polygons, box and whisker plots (including outliers), cumulative frequency diagrams
Interpret scatter diagrams and regression lines for bivariate data; informal interpretation of correlation; correlation does not imply causation

subtopics.subtopicName: Measures of central tendency and variation
subtopics.content: Interpret measures: mean, median, mode; variance, standard deviation, range, interpercentile ranges; calculate standard deviation from summary statistics
Recognise and interpret possible outliers; select or critique data presentation techniques; clean data including missing data, errors and outliers
Understand and use mutually exclusive and independent events; link to discrete and continuous distributions; area under curve represents probability for continuous distributions
Understand and use discrete probability distributions including binomial distribution as a model; identify discrete uniform distribution; notation X~B(n,p); use calculator for binomial probabilities
Understand and apply language: null hypothesis, alternative hypothesis, significance level, test statistic, 1-tail test, 2-tail test, critical value, critical region, acceptance region, p-value
Conduct statistical hypothesis test for proportion in binomial distribution; interpret results in context; understand significance level as probability of incorrectly rejecting null hypothesis
Understand and use fundamental quantities and units: length, time, mass; derived quantities: velocity, acceleration, force, weight; convert between units
Understand and use: position, displacement, distance travelled, velocity, speed, acceleration
Understand, use and interpret graphs: displacement-time (gradient = velocity), velocity-time (gradient = acceleration, area = displacement)
Understand, use and derive formulae for constant acceleration (suvat) for motion in a straight line
Use calculus in kinematics: v=dr/dt, a=dv/dt=d²r/dt²; r=∫v dt, v=∫a dt
Understand concept of force; understand and use Newton's first law; normal reaction, tension, thrust, compression, resistance
Understand and use Newton's second law for motion in a straight line; constant acceleration with forces parallel/perpendicular to motion; vector form with i-j or column vectors
Understand and use weight; motion in straight line under gravity; gravitational acceleration g (default 9.8 m/s²)
Understand and use Newton's third law; equilibrium of forces; application to smooth pulleys and connected particles
